entities.description: The ability of pearl millet to resist infection by ergot

entities.description: A method used for field screening to identify resistance

entities.description: The process of evaluating cultivars in the field for disease resistance

entities.name: ARTIFICIALLY INDUCED EPIDEMICS
entities.description: Controlled disease outbreaks created in the field and greenhouse

entities.name: FIELD
entities.description: The outdoor environment where screening and epidemic tests were conducted

entities.name: GREENHOUSE
entities.description: The controlled indoor environment where epidemic tests were conducted

entities.name: SPRAY INOCULATION
entities.description: The application of an aqueous honeydew conidial suspension to panicles

entities.name: HIGH RELATIVE HUMIDITY
entities.description: Environmental condition of >90% humidity required for epidemic creation

entities.name: PANICLE WETNESS
entities.description: Environmental condition of >80% wetness required for epidemic creation

entities.name: MODERATE TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Environmental temperature between 20-30 C required for epidemic creation

entities.name: ERGOT SEVERITY
entities.description: The measure of the extent of ergot infection in cultivars

entities.name: FUNCTIONAL FIELD RESISTANCE
entities.description: The level of resistance that provides protection under natural epidemic conditions

entities.name: NATURAL ERGOT EPIDEMIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: Naturally occurring outbreaks of ergot disease

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TRAITS
